citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
IACUC approved Protocol #25-08 was reviewed. Although the approved protocol includes a rationale for using animals
and includes the number of animals requested for the study (152 animals), there is no rationale for the number of animals
requested.
Per this Section, a proposal to conduct an activity involving animals, or to make a significant change in an ongoing activity
involving animals, must contain a rationale: for involving animals, for the appropriateness of the species, and for the
number of animals to be used. Complete information must be provided in a proposal for animal use for the IACUC to
review the proposed animal use activities and determine that the proposed activities are in accordance with the
requirements in this subchapter of the Animal Welfare Act. The IACUC needs to address this item. Correct by 3/12/26.

The research facility did not submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or before the
extended deadline due date of 12/31/25.
Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching
shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the
Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the
annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total number of animals used and to ensure that
professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals were followed during the previous Federal
fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report. Correct by 2/16/26.
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.31(e)(2) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
IACUC approved Protocol #25-08 was reviewed. Although the approved protocol includes a rationale for using animals 
and includes the number of animals requested for the study (152 animals), there is no rationale for the number of animals 
requested.  
 
Per this Section, a proposal to conduct an activity involving animals, or to make a significant change in an ongoing activity 
involving animals, must contain a rationale: for involving animals, for the appropriateness of the species, and for the 
number of animals to be used. Complete information must be provided in a proposal for animal use for the IACUC to 
review the proposed animal use activities and determine that the proposed activities are in accordance with the 
requirements in this subchapter of the Animal Welfare Act. The IACUC needs to address this item. Correct by 3/12/26. 
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
The research facility did not submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or before the 
extended deadline due date of 12/31/25. 
 
Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching 
shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the 
Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the 
annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total number of animals used and to ensure that 
professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals were followed during the previous Federal 
fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report. Correct by 2/16/26. 
 
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative. 
 
 n
